etymology.description: 米(米) (mǐ, "rice") as semantic + 角(角) as phonetic -> intermediate form 𠧪 -> further corrupted to 西
etymology.interpretation: The character underwent several stages, initially depicting a pictogram for grain or millet with 米(米) (mǐ, "rice") showing that it refers to grain. The phonetic 角(角) contributed to the sound, evolving through 𠧪 before finally becoming 西, which does not directly connect but forms the current appearance.

examples.chinese: 粟是古老的谷物之一(粟是古老的穀物之一)。
examples.pinyin: Sù shì gǔlǎo de gǔwù zhī yī.
examples.english: Millet is one of the ancient grains.

examples.chinese: 在干旱的地区(在乾旱的地區)，粟可以生长得很好(粟可以生長得很好)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gānhàn de dìqū, sù kěyǐ shēngzhǎng de hěn hǎo.
examples.english: In arid regions, millet can grow very well.

examples.chinese: 古代中国人常用粟制作为主食(古代中國人常用粟製作為主食)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài Zhōngguó rén chángyòng sù zhìzuò wéi zhǔshí.
examples.english: Ancient Chinese people often used millet as a staple food.
